name: aligned_zero_size
reason: If alignment to size 0 is trivially true for all addresses, the spec fails to guard against meaningless zero-size alignment checks

name: inv_allows_empty_arch
code:
proof fn phi_2_inv_allows_empty_arch(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.layers.len() == 0,
    ensures
        arch.inv(),
{
}
reason: If an architecture with zero layers satisfies inv(), then page table reasoning becomes vacuous and no translations are possible

name: upper_vaddr_no_progress
code:
proof fn phi_3_upper_vaddr_no_progress(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.upper_vaddr(layer, base) == base,
{
}
reason: If upper_vaddr equals base, the layer maps zero address space, making the entire page table level useless

name: entry_size_uniform_across_layers
code:
proof fn phi_4_entry_size_uniform_across_layers(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        arch.layers.len() >= 2,
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(0) == arch.entry_size(1),
{
}
reason: If adjacent layers have identical entry sizes, the hierarchical decomposition is broken and multi-level translation provides no granularity benefit

name: entry_base_non_monotonic
code:
proof fn phi_5_entry_base_non_monotonic(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat, j: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i < j,
        j <= arch.num_entries(layer),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, j) <= arch.entry_base(layer, base, i),
{
}
reason: If a higher index yields a lower-or-equal entry base address, the virtual address space ordering is violated and page table walks cannot correctly partition memory
